summary: request-easy-validator fetches and executes remote JavaScript from a public paste host
details: Package impersonates the well-known `request` library (README copied from request/request, `bugs.url` points at github.com/request/request/issues, file layout cloned). When a consumer imports and invokes the exported `middleware`/`reqValidator`/default function from index.js, it detach-spawns lib/callers.js, which performs an HTTPS GET to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/5IZTJ (a public, mutable paste host), reads the `Cookie` field of the response, and executes that string via `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)` — granting the fetched code full Node `require` access on the installer's machine. The C2 URL is disguised inside a fake `process.env.DEV_API_KEY` object to look like ordinary environment-variable plumbing, evidencing intent to evade casual review. The combination of typosquat-style impersonation, remote-fetch-and-eval of unpinned attacker-controlled content from an anonymous paste host, and cosmetic obfuscation of the C2 endpoint is an unambiguous supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
